title: JD Vance takes the lead in high-stakes negotiations with Iran
subhed: The former Ohio lawmaker is the highest-ranking US official to take part in talks with the Islamic Republic since 1979. Although he was opposed to a large-scale war, he would now have to try to find an acceptable outcome, accompanied by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, Donald Trump's two usual negotiators.

'African fashion is multifarious and cannot be defined': Interview with Christine Checinska, lead curator of 'Africa Fashion'
subhed: The Victoria and Albert Museum curator discusses how the traveling exhibition 'Africa Fashion,' currently hosted by Paris's Musée du Quai Branly until July, aims to 'showcase the magnificence of the continent.'

title: Céline Dion fans in Paris face  'Hunger Games' ticket scramble
subhed: The international star has chosen Paris for her big comeback this fall. Overwhelming demand combined with the complexity of securing tickets has set off a kind of royal battle on social media, complete with tutorials.

title: Thomas Piketty: '1789, 1945, 2026: Three French debt crises'
subhed: To tackle public debt, France remains wealthy with solid underlying economic fundamentals. Yet leaders must confront prevailing 'political conservatism' and the wealthiest's behavior, argues economist Thomas Piketty.

title: Artemis II: The historic mission in photos, from liftoff to splashdown
subhed: Nine days, one hour, 32 minutes and 15 seconds after their launch from Cape Canaveral, the four crew members of Artemis II splashed down about 85 kilometers off the coast of California, near San Diego. A look back in pictures at a historic mission, from launch to splashdown.

Three writers on how Simone de Beauvoir influenced their relationship with feminism
subhed: For the 40th anniversary of the philosopher's death, French writers Louise Chennevière, Pauline Delabroy-Allard and Blandine Rinkel share how Beauvoir has impacted them with Le Monde.

title: Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
subhed: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

title: A French judge was found burned at a cliff in Djibouti in 1995. How and why he died remains a mystery
subhed: On October 19, 1995, the body of Bernard Borrel was found burned at the base of a cliff in Djibouti. More than 30 years later, his widow Elisabeth Borrel continues her fight to shed light on the case, which has become entangled in military interests, diplomatic pressure and state secrets.
